role_title: Product Manager, Investment Reporting - Vice President

level_signals: 8 years of experience in product management
Lead and manage a small team of product managers
Engage executive management for ideas and resolutions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8 years of experience in product management, preferably within fintech, wealth management, or related industries
Strong analytical skills with a proven track record of solving complex, ambiguous problems and pushing initiatives to conclusions through innovative solutions
Proven track record of managing technical teams, sprints, and projects, organizing requests and prioritizing, ultimately driving a roadmap
Detail-oriented, with the ability to write comprehensive user stories and acceptance criteria
Strong interpersonal skills and capable of building relationships to gain buy in and achieve business results
Experience with or willingness to become an expert in the alternative investments space, understanding both the product intricacies and wider industry to drive informed decision making
Experience with agile methodologies and a mindset that embraces quick deployment, learning, and iteration
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage executive management for ideas and resolutions
A customer-centric approach, with experience engaging directly with clients to gather insights and feedback and a knack for quick and thoughtful problem solving
Lead and manage a small team of product managers to collectively drive successful outcomes.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with alternative investment reporting and interactions with advisors and advisor teams is preferred

scope.team_size_estimate: small team of product managers (size not specified)
scope.surface: Post-investment advisor platform experience, primarily Investment Dashboard
scope.business_model: B2B fintech platform serving wealth management advisors and advisor teams
primary_metrics_inferred: User adoption of Investment Dashboard features
core_responsibilities: Lead engagements with internal stakeholders and end users to identify and deliver solutions to core functional areas and user experiences
Define product requirements by writing detailed user stories and acceptance criteria for software engineers
Manage the end-to-end lifecycle of product feature development from conception to launch, including testing and iteration
Lead and manage a small team of product managers to collectively drive successful outcomes
Own the product roadmap for specific features or segments of the platform, determining prioritization and ensuring that deliverables are met on time and within scope
Communicate effectively with business stakeholders to gather requirements, provide updates, and align product vision and strategy
Investigate bugs and identify tactical solutions to production issues impacting users
Monitor the performance of product features post-launch and continuously seek opportunities for improvement
Maintain awareness of the competitive landscape from a business, legal and compliance perspective

green_flags: Equity offered to all full-time employees
Comprehensive benefits including 100% employer-paid dental, vision, telemedicine, and virtual mental health counseling
Clear product surface and ownership area (Investment Dashboard / post-investment experience)
Defined hybrid schedule with Friday remote flexibility
Room to grow and broaden responsibilities as company grows
red_flags: Office required Monday–Thursday; only Friday remote — limited flexibility for fully remote candidates
City/location not specified in JD body, making it unclear which office this is tied to
8-year minimum experience floor is high and non-negotiable per JD language
Alternative investments domain is niche and may be difficult to bridge quickly despite 'willingness to learn' framing

fields_inferred_not_stated: scope.business_model (inferred from product description and industry context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
seniority.level (inferred from VP title, years requirement, people management responsibility, and comp range)
location.cities_listed (no city mentioned in JD body; left empty)
ambiguities: The 8-year requirement is framed as '8 years of experience in product management, preferably within fintech, wealth management, or related industries' — the industry preference is soft but the years floor reads as hard. Treated years as hard, industry as context.
No office city or location is specified in the JD body, making it impossible to confirm where the hybrid schedule applies.

'Experience with or willingness to become an expert in the alternative investments space' — the 'willingness' framing softens this, but it appears in the main Qualifications list without 'preferred' language. Treated as hard with bridgeable: yes.
Visa sponsorship policy not mentioned; marked unclear.
